compiled_state.title: Move version bumping from commit time to merge time
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Context
DSO plugin developers working in parallel worktrees experience guaranteed merge conflicts on plugins/dso/.claude-plugin/plugin.json because every commit bumps the patch version, and every worktree modifies the same single-line field. This conflict occurs on every sprint where two or more worktrees are active simultaneously. Moving the bump to merge time (inside merge-to-main.sh, after pulling latest main) eliminates these conflicts entirely since merges are already serialized by the existing lock file. The merge-time bump reads the version from main after pulling (not from the worktree's copy), ensuring each bump builds on the latest version.

## Success Criteria
- Two worktree sessions that both modify code files can merge to main sequentially: both merges exit 0, plugin.json contains no conflict markers, and the final version is exactly two patch increments above the pre-merge baseline
- Every merge to main where version.file_path is configured produces exactly one version increment (patch by default, minor for epic completions via --bump minor flag); when version.file_path is absent, the bump phase is skipped silently with no error
- merge-to-main.sh accepts a --bump flag (patch default, minor for epic completion); the version bump phase reads the current version from main after pull, increments it, commits the result, and continues to the push phase
- tag-release.sh continues to set explicit versions in plugin.json without depending on or invoking merge-to-main.sh — it remains a standalone release tool
- COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b (patch version bump) is removed entirely — no version-related logic remains in the commit workflow
- Sprint skills epic-completion minor bump (Step 13) is replaced with passing --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh

## Dependencies
None. Soft coordination note: Redesign merge state detection epic (5ee9-143e) also modifies merge-to-main.sh — ideally this epic lands before or after, not during, that redesign.

## Approach
Add a new version_bump phase to merge-to-main.sh (between merge and push). Remove version bumping from COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b and sprint skill Step 13. The new phase uses the existing bump-version.sh script, reading the version from the just-pulled main.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Epic complete: all 3 stories closed, all implementation tasks done.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
